title: Authentication & Session Management
principle: Store passwords only as slow, salted adaptive hashes (bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id) — never plaintext, MD5, or SHA-256. On every successful login regenerate the session identifier to defeat session fixation, and set the cookie HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite. Give sessions an idle and absolute timeout, and invalidate them server-side on logout.

snippets.library: argon2-cffi or bcrypt; regenerate the session on login
snippets.vulnerable:
if user.password == request.form['password']:  # plaintext compare
    session['uid'] = user.id
snippets.secure:
from argon2 import PasswordHasher
ph = PasswordHasher()
# at signup: user.hash = ph.hash(password)
ph.verify(user.hash, password)          # raises on mismatch
session.clear(); session.regenerate()   # new session id after auth
session['uid'] = user.id
snippets.note: Compare with the hasher's verify (constant-time), never ==. Set SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY, _SECURE, and _SAMESITE. Rotate the id on privilege change too.
bcrypt.compare / argon2.verify are constant-time. Always regenerate() after login; set maxAge for an absolute timeout.

snippets.library: Spring Security BCryptPasswordEncoder + session fixation protection
snippets.note: Keep Spring's default session-fixation protection on. Mark the cookie HttpOnly/Secure and set server.servlet.session.timeout.

snippets.library: golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt + a fresh session token on login
snippets.note: bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword is constant-time. Mint a new session id from crypto/rand on login and drop the old one; set an expiry.
